classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

ccel.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

title: Three Moral Symbols Devised by the Manichæans for No Good.

source.title: NPNF1-04. Augustine: The Writings Against the Manichaeans and Against the Donatists

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: Now that I have shown, as I think, how much darkness and error is in your opinions about good and evil things in general, let us examine now those three symbols which you extol so highly, and boast of as excellent observances.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: What then are those three symbols?

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: That of the mouth, that of the hands, and that of the breast.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: What does this mean?

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: That man, we are told, should be pure and innocent in mouth, in hands, and in breast.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: But what if he sins with eyes, ears, or nose?

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: What if he hurts some one with his heels, or perhaps kills him?

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: How can he be reckoned criminal when he has not sinned with mouth, hands, or breast?

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: But, it is replied, by the mouth we are to understand all the organs of sense in the head; by the hands, all bodily actions; by the breast, all lustful tendencies.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: To what, then, do you assign blasphemies?

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: To the mouth or to the hand?

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: For blasphemy is an action of the tongue.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: And if all actions are to be classed under one head, why should you join together the actions of the hands and the feet, and not those of the tongue.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: Do you wish to separate the action of the tongue, as being for the purpose of expressing something, from actions which are not for this purpose, so that the symbol of the hands should mean abstinence from all evil actions which are not for the purpose of expressing something?

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: But then, what if some one sins by expressing something with his hands, as is done in writing or in some significant gesture?

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: This cannot be assigned to the tongue and the mouth, for it is done by the hands.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: When you have three symbols of the mouth, the hands, and the breast, it is quite inadmissible to charge against the mouth sins found in the hands.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: And if you assign action in general to the hands, there is no reason for including under this the action of the feet and not that of the tongue.

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: Do you see how the desire of novelty, with its attendant error, lands you in great difficulties?

verses.book: On the Morals of the Manichæans.
verses.text: For you find it impossible to include purification of all sins in these three symbols, which you set forth as a kind of new classification.
